name: Corn Quiche
recipeIngredient: 1 frozen Pet-Ritz (9-inch) deep dish pie shell
1 can whole kernel white corn, drained (12 oz.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw pie shell at room temperature for about 10 to 15 minutes.
2. Prick the bottom and sides of the crust with a fork.
3. Bake the crust on a cookie sheet at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 5 minutes.
4. Reduce oven temperature to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Place corn in the pastry shell and sprinkle with cheese.
6. Combine eggs, half and half, salt, pepper, and nutmeg in a bowl.
7. Pour the egg mixture over the corn and cheese.
8. Drizzle melted butter over the top of the quiche.
9. Sprinkle combined brown sugar and cinnamon on top.
10. Return to oven for 30 minutes or until the center is firm.
Comment: Quiche can be made ahead and frozen. If so, omit topping and the last 5 minutes of baking time. Defrost and bring quiche to room temperature; add topping and bake in a preheated 350 degrees Fahrenheit oven for 15 minutes.
